skill.name: doc-consistency-reviewer
skill.description: Reviews project documentation against source code, schemas, configuration, and contracts to find outdated or inconsistent claims.
skill.summary: Finds mismatches between documentation and implementation with evidence-based severity levels.
skill.author: hylarucoder

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell backticks, weak cryptography, and obfuscation were evaluated as false positives from Markdown fences, example text, and multilingual documentation. No prompt injection, network access, executable script, or secret handling was found. The only retained risk is low because the skill asks the agent to write a local doc-consistency.md report.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static shell-execution alerts point to Markdown fences and inline path formatting. These locations document report templates and examples, and they do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain Markdown code fences or inline code markers in documentation files. No Ruby, shell invocation, command substitution, or runtime script path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts point to skill descriptions and prior evaluation prose, not cryptographic functions or algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive text about documentation review. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, cryptographic APIs, or password hashing behavior.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Alerts Are Plain Documentation
low_findings.description: The high-entropy alerts point to normal Markdown headings and YAML frontmatter. The files are readable text, not binary or encrypted payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files render as ordinary Markdown and YAML frontmatter. The entropy heuristic appears triggered by Chinese text and punctuation, with no encoded blob or hidden payload.

low_findings.title: Local Report File Output Requested
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to output a doc-consistency.md report. This is expected behavior, but it may create or overwrite a local report file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 94 directly asks for a report file output. The action is scoped and transparent, but it is still filesystem interaction that users should expect.

content.value_statement: Documentation often drifts away from real code behavior. This skill compares docs with implementation evidence and produces a prioritized consistency report.
actual_capabilities: Compares README and docs content with source code behavior.
Checks API claims against schemas, contracts, and type definitions.
Reviews configuration, environment variable, and script documentation.
Flags security documentation that conflicts with actual settings.
Produces severity-ranked findings with document and code evidence.
Suggests minimal fixes for each documented inconsistency.
limitations: It does not prove runtime behavior unless the assistant also runs tests.
It depends on accessible source files, schemas, and documentation.
It may need project-specific guidance for unusual architecture.
It can write a local report file when the user permits file changes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Documentation
use_cases.description: Check that public README files and guides match the current implementation before a release.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Request Docs
use_cases.description: Verify that documentation changes in a pull request match the related code changes.

use_cases.title: Audit API Reference Accuracy
use_cases.description: Compare API docs with schemas, route handlers, and type definitions to find stale contract details.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Documentation Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of standard project docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Review README.md and docs/ for claims that do not match the current code. List each issue with evidence and severity.

prompt_templates.title: Configuration Accuracy Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of setup and environment docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare setup, environment variable, and script documentation with the actual configuration files and package scripts. Report mismatches.

prompt_templates.title: API Contract Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify API documentation against route handlers, schemas, OpenAPI files, and type definitions. Prioritize breaking mismatches.

prompt_templates.title: Full Consistency Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a complete documentation consistency audit across README, docs, contracts, configuration, security notes, and examples. Write the findings to doc-consistency.md.

output_examples.input: Review our README before the next release.
output: P1: The README describes a setup command that no longer exists in the project scripts.
P2: The documented default port differs from the configuration file.
P3: A guide links to a moved troubleshooting page.

output_examples.input: Check whether our API docs match the implementation.
output: P1: The response field names in the API reference do not match the route output.
P1: A required request parameter is documented as optional.
P2: One documented error status is missing from the handler behavior.
P0: The security guide claims a protection is enabled, but the configuration shows it disabled.
P1: The role permissions table omits an access check used by the code.
P2: The deployment guide does not mention a required security header.

best_practices: Provide the relevant source files, schemas, and documentation in the same review context.
Ask for evidence lines so each finding can be verified quickly.
Resolve P0 and P1 findings before release or external publication.
anti_patterns: Using the skill as a substitute for test execution or runtime validation.
Accepting a finding without checking the cited document and code evidence.
Reviewing only README files when API, configuration, or security docs also exist.

faq.question: Does this skill change my documentation automatically?
faq.answer: No. It reviews documentation and may write a report file when requested.

faq.question: What documents does it review?
faq.answer: It focuses on README files, docs directories, API docs, configuration docs, examples, and security notes.

faq.question: What does it use as the source of truth?
faq.answer: It treats source code, configuration, schemas, contracts, and type definitions as primary evidence.

faq.question: Can it review API documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare documented endpoints, parameters, fields, and errors with implementation evidence.

faq.question: Does it execute project code?
faq.answer: No execution is required by the skill. Runtime checks need a separate user request.

faq.question: How should teams use the report?
faq.answer: Teams should fix high-severity mismatches first, then update examples, links, and lower-severity wording issues.
